challenges.title: Stack Implementation
challenges.description: Implement a `Stack` class with methods: `push(item)` to add to the top, `pop()` to remove and return the top item (raise IndexError if empty), `peek()` to return the top item without removing it (raise IndexError if empty), `is_empty()` to return True if the stack has no items, and `__len__` to return the stack size.
challenges.starter_code:
class Stack:
    """A last-in, first-out (LIFO) data structure."""

    def __init__(self):
        # Your code here
        pass

    def push(self, item):
        """Push an item onto the top of the stack."""
        # Your code here
        pass

    def pop(self):
        """Remove and return the top item. Raises IndexError if empty."""
        # Your code here
        pass

    def peek(self):
        """Return the top item without removing it. Raises IndexError if empty."""
        # Your code here
        pass

    def is_empty(self):
        """Return True if the stack is empty."""
        # Your code here
        pass

    def __len__(self):
        """Return the number of items in the stack."""
        # Your code here
        pass
challenges.test_code:
s = Stack()
assert s.is_empty() is True
assert len(s) == 0
s.push(10)
s.push(20)
s.push(30)
assert s.is_empty() is False
assert len(s) == 3
assert s.peek() == 30
assert len(s) == 3
assert s.pop() == 30
assert s.pop() == 20
assert len(s) == 1
assert s.peek() == 10
assert s.pop() == 10
assert s.is_empty() is True
try:
    s.pop()
    assert False, 'Should have raised IndexError'
except IndexError:
    pass
try:
    s.peek()
    assert False, 'Should have raised IndexError'
except IndexError:
    pass
s.push('a')
s.push('b')
assert s.pop() == 'b'
assert s.pop() == 'a'
hints: Use a Python list as the internal storage. push() is list.append(), pop() is list.pop().
For peek(), return self._items[-1] but check for empty first.

challenges.title: Binary Search Tree
challenges.description: Implement a `BinaryTree` class with an internal `_Node` class. Support `insert(value)` to add values maintaining BST order (left < parent < right), and `inorder_traversal()` that returns a list of values in sorted order. Also implement `__contains__(value)` for `in` operator support and `__len__` to return the total number of nodes.
challenges.test_code:
tree = BinaryTree()
assert len(tree) == 0
tree.insert(5)
tree.insert(3)
tree.insert(7)
tree.insert(1)
tree.insert(4)
tree.insert(6)
tree.insert(8)
assert tree.inorder_traversal() == [1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8]
assert len(tree) == 7
assert 5 in tree
assert 3 in tree
assert 8 in tree
assert 99 not in tree
assert 0 not in tree
tree2 = BinaryTree()
tree2.insert(10)
assert tree2.inorder_traversal() == [10]
assert len(tree2) == 1
assert 10 in tree2
tree2.insert(5)
tree2.insert(15)
tree2.insert(3)
assert tree2.inorder_traversal() == [3, 5, 10, 15]
assert len(tree2) == 4
hints: Store a _root node (initially None) and a _size counter. Insert recursively: if value < node.value, go left; else go right.
For inorder_traversal, use a helper that recursively visits left, appends value, then visits right.
